title: DevOps PQC Workshop — All Industries, All Regions
whatToExpect: Operational PQC concerns — TLS at scale, KMS rotation, HSM migration, gateway readiness
Module walkthroughs for ops-quantum-impact, network/VPN/SSH/web-gateway PQC, KMS, HSM, platform engineering
Page tours for /timeline, /compliance, /migrate — the deadlines and vendors that pace your runbook

steps.title: What you will leave with
steps.whyItMatters: A runbook-ready view of which operational layers need PQC migration first.
steps.expectedOutput: A ranked list of operational layers (KMS, HSM, gateways, TLS) by migration urgency.
steps.narration: This workshop walks DevOps through the operational layers that need PQC migration — TLS at scale, KMS key rotation, HSM hardware migration, gateway and VPN readiness. By the end you have a ranked runbook of layers to address and the vendor catalog to source replacements from.

cues.text: DevOps workshop — TLS at scale, KMS rotation, HSM migration, gateway readiness.

steps.title: Confirm DevOps persona
steps.whyItMatters: The persona drives operational deep links and recommendations.
steps.narration: Set the persona once — the platform tunes every page to operational concerns.

cues.text: Persona definition — four picks shape every page. Ops drives this flow.
Opening the persona wizard.
Step 1 — Track. Technical track unlocks developer, architect, ops, and researcher roles.
Step 2 — Role. Ops surfaces the modules and tools tuned to your job.
Step 3 — Region. Filters timeline, leaders, threats and compliance to your jurisdiction.
Step 4 — Industry. Surfaces industry-specific threats, frameworks and vendors.
These four facets shape every workshop step. Change any one to re-personalize.

steps.title: /timeline — operational deadline planning
steps.whyItMatters: The deadline runway — what your runbooks need to address and by when.
tasks: Note the key affordances called out by the workshop
steps.narration: The /timeline page lists every regulatory and standards deadline. Filter by your country to see your operational runway.

cues.text: Global PQC migration timeline — every deadline operations needs to plan against.
NIST. Hybrid TLS by 2030. Legacy disallowed by 2035. CCCS Canada. High-priority systems by 2031. ASD Australia. End of 2030.
Filter to your country to see jurisdiction-specific deadlines.

steps.title: /compliance — frameworks and operational impact
steps.whyItMatters: Which frameworks gate your operational changes — PCI, ISM, CCCS, FedRAMP.
tasks: Note the key affordances called out by the workshop
steps.expectedOutput: Familiarity with /compliance — frameworks and operational impact
steps.narration: The /compliance page maps frameworks to operational requirements — what audits will check, what evidence you need to retain.

cues.text: Compliance frameworks with PQC requirements — what audits will check.
PCI DSS version 4 section 12 dot 3 dot 3 forces a crypto inventory. Your configuration management database needs post-quantum fields.
ASD ISM-1917 prohibits classical crypto end-2030. Operational deadline is firm.

steps.title: /migrate — operational vendor catalog
steps.whyItMatters: Vendor products ranked by PQC readiness — pick replacements for your KMS, HSM, gateways.
tasks: Note the key affordances called out by the workshop
steps.narration: The /migrate catalog filters vendor products by infrastructure layer. Use it to source replacements for your operational stack.

cues.text: /migrate — operational vendor catalog. Filter by layer to find replacement candidates.
Hardware layer — HSMs and secure elements with PQC support. Entrust nShield is the verified leader.
Network layer — gateways, firewalls, load balancers with PQC TLS support.
Filter by Verified status only to shortlist production-ready vendors.

steps.title: Quiz — test what you learned
steps.whyItMatters: A 5-to-45-minute quiz with persona-tuned topics gives you a quick measure of what stuck.
tasks: Note the duration slider and difficulty filter
See how persona-tuned topics are pre-selected
Run the quiz yourself after the workshop
steps.expectedOutput: A working knowledge of the quiz interface; you'll come back to actually take it.
steps.narration: The quiz is the quickest way to check what you've absorbed. Pick how much time you have, pick the topics that matter, and get a scored breakdown by category and difficulty.

cues.text: Test what you learned. Pick a duration, pick topics, go.
Duration slider. Five to forty-five minutes. Quiz scales the question count to match.
Difficulty filter. Beginner, Intermediate, Advanced. Multi-select.
Topic grid. Categories pre-filtered by your persona context.
Selected topics show a checkmark. The Start button activates once you pick at least one.
Start the quiz when you're ready. We'll skip running it inside the workshop.
Results show your score, category breakdown, difficulty breakdown, and let you retake or change topics.

steps.title: Recap and next steps
steps.whyItMatters: Three operational items to file in your runbook today.
steps.expectedOutput: A short ranked list of operational layers needing migration.
steps.narration: Three operational takeaways: deadline-driven prioritization, KMS+HSM as your highest-blast-radius migrations, and crypto agility patterns to roll out without breaking prod.

cues.text: Three ops takeaways: deadlines, KMS+HSM as biggest blast radius, agility patterns.
